reason_for_decision: The article passes as a foundation explainer because it defines clutter clearly, shows why clutter matters operationally, and distinguishes clutter from noise and interference.
strengths: The article explains clutter in plain language without losing the important operational consequences for small or slow targets.
It is especially useful on the role of geometry and local site tuning in clutter behavior.
The distinction between clutter, noise, and interference is clear and beginner-friendly.
summary_assessment: This is a solid pass for a foundation article. It is readable, technically sound, and gives newcomers an accurate mental model for why clutter dominates many radar problems.
